reasoning: Stock prices over short horizons approximate a random walk with slight positive drift. AT&T is a low-beta, high-dividend stock, so price appreciation (without dividends) is relatively slow. Over short periods like one week or one month, the probability of the price being strictly higher is very close to 50%, perhaps slightly higher (e.g., 51-52%) due to equity risk premium. Over the three-month horizon until January 2026, AT&T will likely pay out a dividend (historically dropping the stock price upon the ex-dividend date in early January), which negates the positive background drift in the raw price. Per the formatting instructions, uncertainty increases and estimates tend closer to 0.5 for more distant forecasting horizons. Therefore, the estimates scale gradually back to exactly 50%.
